sections.title: Arboreal Adaptation
content: Treecko has small hooks on the bottom of its feet that enable it to scale vertical walls. It is known for its cool and collected personality.
It makes its home in large trees and will fiercely defend its territory from anyone who approaches.

sections.title: Jungle Predator
content: Sceptile's agility is unmatched in the forest. The seeds on its back are filled with nutrients that can revitalize trees.
The leaves growing on its arms are as sharp as swords, allowing it to slice through thick branches and foes alike.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A high-pressure 'Glass Cannon' in the Great and Ultra Leagues. With Bullet Seed and Leaf Blade, it has one of the fastest charged move activation rates in the game.

sections.title: Pyro-Kinetic Martial Arts
content: Blaziken has incredibly strong legs; it can easily clear a 30-story building in a single leap. Its punches can leave opponents scorched.
In battle, it blows out intense flames from its wrists, which intensify as it faces tougher opponents.

sections.title: Design Origins
content: Inspired by the Shamo breed of Japanese chicken, which was bred specifically for its fighting spirit and upright posture.

sections.title: Hydro-Mechanical Prowess
content: Swampert is very strong and can easily drag a large boulder weighing more than a ton. It has powerful vision that enables it to see even in turbid water.
Its fins can sense subtle changes in the movement of air and water, allowing it to predict storms and incoming waves.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: Widely considered the most dominant 'Mud Shot' user in GBL history. Its access to Hydro Cannon and Earthquake gives it near-perfect coverage across all leagues.

sections.title: Psychic Devotion
content: Gardevoir has the ability to read the future. It is famously protective and will expend all its psychic power to create a small black hole to protect its trainer.
It does not feel the pull of gravity because it supports its body with psychic energy.

sections.title: Economic Energy Management
content: Slaking spends its entire day lounging and eating grass. It only moves when it has absolutely no other choice.
Despite its lazy appearance, it can exert horrifying power by releasing all its pent-up energy at once.

sections.title: Stat Anomaly
content: Slaking has a base stat total higher than many Legendary Pokémon, which is balanced by its 'Truant' ability that forces it to skip every other turn.

sections.title: Subterranean Scavenger
content: Sableye lives in caves where it leads a quiet life. It feeds on gemstones it finds in the earth, which has caused its eyes to transform into gems.
It is feared by many who believe it can steal the spirits of those it gazes upon in the dark.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The 'King of the Safe Swap' in the Great League. Its 'Return' (purified) coverage and Ghost/Dark typing make it extremely difficult to counter without a dedicated Fairy-type.

sections.title: Aerial Soprano
content: Altaria dances and wheels through the sky among billowy, cotton-like clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano voice that puts listeners into a dreamy state.
It is very affectionate; if it bonds with a person, it will envelop them with its soft, fluffy wings and hum.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A foundational pillar of the Great League meta. Its extreme bulk and Dragon Breath pressure allow it to win most neutral matchups comfortably.

sections.title: Aesthetic Pacifism
content: Milotic is said to be the most beautiful of all Pokémon. It has the power to becalm such emotions as anger and hostility to end arguments.
It lives at the bottom of large lakes. When its body glows a vivid pink, it releases a wave of energy that is soothing to the soul.

sections.title: Organic Supercomputer
content: Metagross has four brains that are joined by a complex neural network. As a result, it can perform difficult intellectual calculations faster than a supercomputer.
When it hunts, it pins its prey to the ground under its massive body. It then eats the victim with the mouth on its stomach.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The undisputed master of the Master League Premiere format. Its combination of Meteor Mash and high-defense Steel typing makes it a mandatory inclusion for many teams.

sections.title: Extraterrestrial Metallurgy
content: Registeel's body is made of a strange material that is harder than any metal on Earth. It is hollow inside, and no one knows what it eats.
The metal that composes its body is tempered by pressure deep underground over tens of thousands of years.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: Often cited as the most 'un-fun' Pokémon to face in GBL. Its astronomical bulk and high-damage Lock-On energy generation allow it to spam powerful moves like Zap Cannon and Focus Blast.

sections.title: Abyssal Sovereign
content: Kyogre has the power to control water and create massive rain clouds. It has been referred to as a savior by people who have suffered from droughts.
It is said to have expanded the seas by covering the earth with torrential rains and towering tidal waves.

sections.title: Tectonic Titan
content: Groudon is said to be the personification of the land itself. It has the power to scatter rain clouds and make water evaporate with light and heat.
It has been locked in a traditional clash with Kyogre since ancient times as each sought to expand their respective domain.

sections.title: Ozone Arbiter
content: Rayquaza lived in the ozone layer for hundreds of millions of years without ever descending to the ground. It feeds on water and particles in the atmosphere.
It acts as a mediator in the conflict between Kyogre and Groudon, descending to Earth to quell their destructive battles.
